reason: Response A commits fully to making the sensory and conceptual case and lands an earned thematic final line ("That forgetting is the loveliest thing"), which reads as genuine Sonnet. Response B brackets itself with meta-framing ("a question that pretends to ask for a verdict") and a presumptuous, flattering closer ("I suspect you already knew which one you loved, and only wanted to hear it said well") plus the rigid noun/verb scaffolding — these are the kind of performed-insight tells an imitator over-applies to signal Sonnet's essayist register.
